title: Pelican to Murder — Level 3 Junior Detective
description: Level 3 version with social-media misdirection and cross-app evidence matching.

answerKey.method: Lured Lena to the photo alcove and triggered her allergy by switching garnish oil
answerKey.motive: To stop Lena from exposing a staged charity livestream scam
answerKey.keyEvidence: Raw livestream cache, garnish bottle photo, and deleted DM thread
answerKey.explanation: Brielle used the livestream chaos to create an alibi while raw cache files placed her near the photo alcove. The garnish oil photo and deleted DMs show both the method and motive: Lena had proof that Brielle’s charity promo was staged.

publicClues.title: Case Opened
publicClues.text: Pelican to Murder begins as a live investigation. This session is Level 3: Junior Detective Case.

publicClues.title: Difficulty Loaded
publicClues.text: This is Level 3: Junior Detective Case. Evidence and answers are unique to this level.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 1 Available
publicClues.text: Round 1 is ready. Answer the checkpoint after reviewing the photo alcove.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 2 Available
publicClues.text: Round 2 is ready. Compare timeline movement before answering.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 3 Available
publicClues.text: Round 3 is ready. Digital evidence now matters.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 4 Available
publicClues.text: Round 4 is ready. Find the motive evidence.

publicClues.title: Final Accusation Open
publicClues.text: Final questions are open. Complete all 10 total questions before the lock.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Lena received a “come to the photo wall” call from a blocked number.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: The blocked number matches the recovery contact on Brielle’s creator account.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Lena messaged Brielle: “The charity total does not match what you posted.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: Brielle deleted a reply that said, “Don’t ruin this night for both of us.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Lena’s phone paused at the photo alcove before the collapse.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Brielle’s device briefly left the livestream table and entered the same alcove.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: The charity landing page shows only a small transfer to the named nonprofit.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: A larger payment went to a creator management account linked to Brielle.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A backstage photo shows a garnish oil bottle on Brielle’s prop tray.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: A reflection in the photo wall mirror shows Brielle handing Lena a drink.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: The published livestream skips the alcove handoff.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: The raw cache keeps three seconds showing Brielle moving the garnish bottle.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 1
contacts.text: Brielle’s manager is saved as “Donation Push.”

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: Lena saved the same number under “charity mismatch.”

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: Brielle’s public clip is not the same as the raw cache.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Detective note: the alcove mirror catches what the main camera hides.

files.title: Files Evidence 1
files.text: Deleted DM export shows Brielle knew Lena had the charity numbers.

files.title: Files Evidence 2
files.text: Creator payout file lists the same transaction ID as the disputed donation.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 1
browser.text: Brielle searched “how long livestream cache stays available.”

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2
browser.text: Her device also opened a page about removing metadata from stories.

rounds.dialogue: Round 1 is open. Focus on the photo alcove. The clues in this round are built to test one part of the case logic.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 1 is nearly complete. Lock in what the photo alcove proves before the next round opens.

rounds.objective: Review evidence connected to the raw clip and answer the checkpoint question.
rounds.dialogue: Round 2 is open. Focus on the raw clip. The clues in this round are built to test one part of the case logic.

rounds.objective: Review evidence connected to the deleted dms and answer the checkpoint question.
rounds.dialogue: Round 3 is open. Focus on the deleted dms. The clues in this round are built to test one part of the case logic.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 3 is nearly complete. Lock in what the deleted dms proves before the next round opens.

rounds.title: Round 4: The Charity Scam
rounds.objective: Review evidence connected to the charity scam and answer the checkpoint question.
rounds.dialogue: Round 4 is open. Focus on the charity scam. The clues in this round are built to test one part of the case logic.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 4 is nearly complete. Lock in what the charity scam proves before the next round opens.

rounds.title: Round 5: The Garnish Bottle
rounds.objective: Review evidence connected to the garnish bottle and answer the checkpoint question.
rounds.dialogue: Round 5 is open. Focus on the garnish bottle. The clues in this round are built to test one part of the case logic.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 5 is nearly complete. Lock in what the garnish bottle proves before the next round opens.

rounds.title: Final Accusation
rounds.objective: Answer the final five questions to close the case.
rounds.dialogue: The final accusation window is open. Choose the culprit, method, motive, key evidence, and the statement that fails.
rounds.countdownReview: The final window is closing. Submit all answers before the case locks.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Pelican to Murder. You are playing Level 3: Junior Detective Case. Study each app carefully. Every level has its own truth path, evidence trail, and final answer set.
revealScript: Case closed. This was Level 3: Junior Detective Case. The culprit was Brielle Hart. The method: Lured Lena to the photo alcove and triggered her allergy by switching garnish oil. The motive: To stop Lena from exposing a staged charity livestream scam. The proof: Raw livestream cache, garnish bottle photo, and deleted DM thread.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: Where was Lena lured before the collapse?
Checkpoint 2: Which movement clue breaks the livestream alibi?

options.label: Her device left the livestream table

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 3: Which digital record matters most?
Checkpoint 4: What points toward Brielle’s motive?
Checkpoint 5: Which physical clue supports the method?

options.label: The garnish oil bottle on the prop tray

questions.prompt: Final 2: How was the murder carried out?

options.label: Brielle used garnish oil during the alcove handoff
Tessa keyed the wrong register code
To stop Lena exposing the charity livestream scam

questions.prompt: Final 4: Which evidence best proves it?

options.label: Raw cache, deleted DMs, and garnish photo
Tessa said the glass had a marker
Brielle claimed she never left the livestream table
Cole said he reset the booth
